title: 10 best herbal teas for phlegm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and congestion, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm. Additionally, licorice root contains compounds that reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and lungs, providing relief from a sore or scratchy throat and cough.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Licorice root has been shown to reduce coughing by inhibiting the production of mucus and relieving bronchial inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing a persistent cough.
Expectorant properties: The glycosides present in licorice root help loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and chest tightness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice tea's flavonoids and polyphenols have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, making it an effective natural treatment for respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing coughing and congestion associated with phlegm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat and lungs, making it easier to expel phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhizal root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat one cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop until it reaches a rolling boil then turn off heat.
Add the measured glycyrrhizal root powder to the hot water and stir well to combine ingredients.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes then strain into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool slightly before consuming within the next 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava teas helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up excess phlegm. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce congestion and discomfort associated with respiratory issues. By promoting healthy lung function, herbal kava teas can provide relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Kava tea's antitussive compounds, such as kavalactones, help to soothe and calm coughs, reducing phlegm production and clearing congestion.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in kava tea, like kava pyrones, loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Kava tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to phlegm production and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and saponins, the roots help to break down mucus and reduce congestion.
Rhyzomes: With its thick, woody structure, the rhyzomes are often used to create a soothing, expectorant-rich tea that helps loosen phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Gently crush 2 tablespoons of dried kava root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the kava powder with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger and 1 cinnamon stick in a medium saucepan.
Boil 4 cups of water and slowly pour it over the spice mixture, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large pitcher, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before refrigerating it for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea, made from the herb Echinacea purpurea, contains compounds that help to break down and expel excess mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Additionally, the tea's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat infections that can exacerbate phlegm production, promoting a healthier and more balanced respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea's antimicrobial compounds, such as cinnamic acid and chicoric acid, help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the respiratory tract, reducing phlegm accumulation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols and flavonoids present in Purple Coneflower tea have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and mucus production in the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm.
Mucolytic properties: Echinacea's mucolytic compounds, such as caffeic acid, help break down excess mucus, allowing for its more efficient removal from the respiratory tract and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing congestion and relieving phlegm.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower contain compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it a common ingredient in teas for phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves according to your preferred quantity for the recipe.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried flowers and leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh material into a teapot or infuser.
Heat filtered water to a simmer at around 200°f to 212°f and pour it over the echinacea mixture in the teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes allowing the herbs to release their natural oils and flavonoids into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids, serving hot or at room temperature as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with phlegm because of its natural diuretic properties, which help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the body. The tea's bitter compounds also stimulate digestion and increase saliva production, further aiding in the elimination of phlegm. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm. As a result, drinking dandelion tea can provide relief from congestion and coughs associated with excess mucus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of phlegm buildup.
Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel.

Mucilage content: Dandelion root contains a type of mucilage that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with phlegm buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tracts, making them effective in reducing phlegm production.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits, also known as achenes, contain a gentle expectorant property that helps loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it an effective natural remedy for phlegmy coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers or leaves according to your needs and preferences.
Dry the harvested dandelion flowers or leaves in a warm, dry place for several hours.
Combine one teaspoon of dried dandelion flowers or leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining it and discarding the solids.
Drink the herbal dandelion tea two to three times daily, after meals, for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein teas helps with phlegm because it possesses natural expectorant properties that loosen and clear excess mucus from the respiratory tract. The saponins present in mullein leaves work to thin and break up stubborn phlegm, making it easier for the body to expel. Additionally, mullein's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce congestion and soothe irritated airways, allowing for deeper breathing and improved lung function. This natural remedy can provide relief from coughs, wheezing, and chest congestion caused by respiratory infections or allergies.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mullein tea contains mucilages, which are thick, gel-like substances that can help soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with excess phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Mullein tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in mullein tea have expectorant properties, which means they can help loosen and clear excess mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its expectorant properties, mullein roots help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs.
Stems: The stems of mullein contain mucilages that soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mullein leaves and flowers from the plant verbascum thapsus.
Combine one teaspoon of dried mullein leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh leaves and flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes in a teapot or infuser to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea hot or warm two to three times daily as needed to help alleviate phlegm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort teas helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the lungs and airways. The herb's compounds, such as figwort resin and scutellarein, work to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and promote healthy breathing. Additionally, figwort teas have been shown to break up stubborn phlegm and relieve chest congestion, providing natural relief for those struggling with respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Figwort tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, bronchi, and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and reduce congestion.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Figwort's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, which can relieve discomfort and make breathing easier.

Antimicrobial Properties: Figwort tea contains antimicrobial agents that help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause excessive mucus production, reducing the risk of secondary complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids and saponins, making them effective in breaking down and expelling mucus, reducing inflammation, and relieving congestion associated with phlegm.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing excess mucus production and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh figwort leaves and flowers from a trusted source or purchase dried ones.
Combine one teaspoon of dried figwort with eight ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the flavors and properties to blend.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the tea three times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate phlegm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with phlegm because of its natural decongestant properties, which can effectively relieve congestion and ease breathing. The tea's antispasmodic agents help to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, allowing mucus to be cleared more efficiently. Additionally, the calming effects of English lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with phlegm buildup and respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Lavender tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea may help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as sore throats and coughing.

Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea's antimicrobial activity can help combat infections that contribute to phlegm production, such as sinusitis and bronchitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: English lavender roots are used in herbal teas to help clear mucus and congestion, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of the English lavender plant contain potent oils that help soothe and calm respiratory issues, reducing coughs and expectoration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers in the morning when they are fully open and fragrant weighing approximately 20-25 grams for each serving.
Rinse the lavender flowers gently under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed lavender flowers with one quart of boiling filtered water in a large glass teapot allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids then add honey to taste if desired.
Serve the tea hot or at room temperature after cooling down slightly for best results drinking at least three cups daily to help with phlegm relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with phlegm because its natural oils, such as menthol, have a calming effect on the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. This can help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion, making it easier to expel excess mucus and phlegm from the body. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to thin out mucus, allowing for easier coughing up of phlegm and relieving chest tightness. remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint tea helps to loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint tea's antispasmodic effects help to relax and calm the muscles in the bronchial tubes, reducing spasms that can exacerbate coughing and congestion.
Decongestant properties: Peppermint tea's decongestant properties help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have a calming effect on the respiratory system, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving coughs associated with phlegm.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain compounds that help to break down and expel excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or one quarter cup of fresh peppermint leaves into a heat-resistant infuser.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 200-212°f and pour it over the peppermint leaves in the infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and aromas to release fully.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Serve the warm tea hot, or let it cool to room temperature and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with phlegm because of its natural decongestant properties, which allow it to ease respiratory congestion and clear out excess mucus from the lungs. The herb's antimicrobial compounds also help combat infections that can cause phlegm buildup. Additionally, rosemary tea's warm and soothing nature provides relief from coughs and sinus pressure, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory issues like bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Rosemary tea helps loosen and clear phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to cough up, by stimulating the production of mucus-thinning substances.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic bronchitis and other respiratory issues that cause excess phlegm.

Bronchodilatory properties: Rosemary tea has been shown to relax the airway muscles, which helps open up the airways and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are valued for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried rosemary leaves from your garden or local market.
Rinse the rosemary leaves with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its essential oils.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to drinking temperature within 10-15 minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, also help to reduce inflammation and combat infections that can contribute to excess phlegm production. By soothing the throat and airways, thyme teas can provide relief from congestion and coughing, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, sinusitis, and bronchial issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause excessive mucus production and phlegm buildup in the throat and lungs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to excess mucus production and phlegm accumulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help reduce excess mucus production and alleviate coughs, making them suitable for making teas to relieve phlegm-related symptoms.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain antispasmodic compounds that can help soothe the respiratory tract and ease congestion, making them a valuable addition to teas aimed at clearing phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thyme leaves from your garden or store-bought herb in sufficient quantities for your desired tea batch size.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried thyme leaves or two tablespoons of fresh thyme leaves per 8 ounce cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured thyme leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and steep for 5 to 7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard any excess plant material.
Add honey to taste and stir well before drinking immediately as a warm tea remedy for phlegm relief.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing phlegm production.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and fatty acids that help to break down and expel phlegm, relieving congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers.
Combine the measured foeniculum vulgare with one cup of boiling water.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to thin and loosen mucus in the chest. Antimicrobial property: Ginger's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause phlegm buildup and sinusitis, promoting a healthy respiratory system.

Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant contain compounds that help to loosen and expel phlegm, making it a popular ingredient in cough drops and teas.
remedy_recipe: Wash fresh ginger root under cold running water then chop into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Measure one teaspoon of chopped ginger and steep it in boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup to remove solids.
Add honey to taste as desired and stir well to dissolve while still warm.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers according to your preference.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried herb for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a heat-resistant cup or teapot for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Consume the tea warm or at room temperature immediately after preparation.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis for every eight ounces of water needed.

Steep the marshmallow root in the boiling water for seven to ten minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids.

remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers for every cup of boiling water.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the loose herbs.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha spicata leaves from your garden or local market in the morning for maximum potency.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for phlegm are a natural remedy that helps to alleviate congestion, coughs, and colds by soothing and thinning out excess mucus in the body. These teas work by using herbs such as thyme, ginger, and eucalyptus to gently calm and clear airways, making it easier to breathe. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals can experience relief from sinus pressure, chest congestion, and coughing fits, ultimately improving their overall quality of life and ability to function normally.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat phlegm with herbal teas is to brew a strong decoction of slippery elm, which helps to soothe and thin out excess mucus. Combine it with ginger tea, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that ease congestion. Thyme tea also adds its antimicrobial benefits to combat underlying infections. Drink these teas hot throughout the day to help break up phlegm and promote a productive cough.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to phlegm that are treated with herbal teas are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Herbs like thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint help to clear congestion and ease breathing by loosening mucus. Ginger tea also soothes the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of bronchitis and pneumonia. Additionally, herbs like slippery elm and licorice root calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief for sinusitis and postnasal drip.
